name: Terry McLaurin
headline: Injury-marred 2025 dims a still-dangerous deep threat
body:
McLaurin endured a frustrating, abbreviated 2025: just 10 games and 76.2 fantasy points, a steep fall from his 2024 breakout when he ranked WR12 with 1,096 yards, 13 touchdowns and 185.8 points across 17 games. The volume cratered with availability, his target share slipping to 13.9 percent from 23.2, and routes dropping from 478 to 228. Yet the per-rep efficiency stayed elite: a 0.529 EPA per play, 2.55 yards per route run and a 61.7 success rate actually topped his stellar 2024 marks.

The 2019 third-rounder (Pick 76, Ohio State) remains Washington number one and a true deep threat, with two 50-plus catches and a 56-yard long despite the injury. At 29 with Jayden Daniels feeding him, a healthy 2026 should restore double-digit touchdown upside and a 22-plus percent target share. The boom/bust label persists, but his floor and ceiling justify a confident WR2 draft slot and weekly starter status as long as he is on the field.
